mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate lower corporate discount rates and borrowing costs, directly increasing the present value of future equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer demand and elevated recession risk, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in over 60 percent of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand recovery, driving positive quarterly equity returns through higher corporate revenues.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rising interest rates, positive industrial production growth confirms resilient manufacturing output, supporting short-term equity price appreciation despite tighter financial conditions.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth during tightening cycles yields negative 5-day forward returns in over 65 percent of observations, or regime interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects stronger corporate earnings fundamentals and economic expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as equity valuations adjust upward.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid consumer price inflation exceeding 0.4 percent monthly forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL monthly change crosses above 0.4 percent
failure_condition: High inflation months are followed by positive 20-day returns in over 60 percent of cases, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for Fed forward guidance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, yielding positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of instances, or interaction term lacks statistical power.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, diverging real consumption growth from real GDP indicates unsustainable household leverage, triggering short-term equity sell-offs as recession probabilities rise.
failure_condition: Consumption-GDP divergence during late cycles produces positive 5-day returns in over 65 percent of cases, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for credit spreads.
